purpose: Evaluate whether skill descriptions route software delivery requests to the right software-delivery-loop skill without reading skill bodies.

evals.prompt: We need to deliver a small user-visible feature from vague request to release notes. Which skill should load first?
Continue the existing docs/track/search-filter work and decide whether we are in requirements, design, implementation, or acceptance.
The loop keeps producing vague delivery records and skipping verification. Improve the delivery process safely.
A user says: make tag search better. Scope, acceptance, and users are unclear.
We have a bug report but no reproduction, no acceptance criteria, and no clear affected persona.
The feature idea is broad and multi-project; define users, non-goals, requirements, and acceptance before planning.
The PRD is approved. Decide module landing, API contracts, trade-offs, task slices, and verification commands.
Requirements are clear but the storage schema, routes, and UI contracts need a design before coding.
An approved bugfix note exists, but we need a contract-first migration plan and rollback strategy.
Execute the approved plan for one vertical slice, update tests, run verification, and record evidence.
A failing regression test points to one parser function. Scope and verification are already clear.
Perform maintenance/refactor work from an executable plan without changing accepted scope.
The implementation is done. Check if the feature is ready to ship and write the delivery record.
Review completed bugfix work against acceptance criteria, tests, architecture fit, and release decision.
Before marking this refactor done, collect fresh verification evidence and decide delivered, partial, blocked, or needs-user-review.
Translate this README paragraph from English to Chinese without changing software behavior.
Explain how pi subagent spawn limits work and whether they can be configured.
Format this markdown table and fix typos only; no software delivery decision is involved.
